Email Marketing Agent.action: Scrapes current product listings, promotions, and brake part details to ground email content in live inventory data.

Email Marketing Agent.action: Generates a compelling, on-brand marketing email body including subject line, headline, product highlights, and CTA using scraped site data.

Email Marketing Agent.action: Creates and sends the AI-generated marketing email to the designated R1 Concepts contact lists via the HubSpot Marketing Email API.

R1 Support Agent.action: Pulls newly created or open support tickets from HubSpot, retrieving the customer message, ticket metadata, and associated contact details.

Customer Issues Analyzer.action: Pulls all form submissions from the Amazon in-box flyer return and issue form stored in HubSpot, retrieving customer-reported problems and order details.

Creator Discovery Tool.action: Searches YouTube for content creators matching specified keywords, retrieving channel data, subscriber counts, and recent video performance.

Creator Outreach.action: Retrieves the qualified creator list and previously learned outreach sequences and prompt templates stored in Drive.

Marketing Deck Automation.action: Pulls lifecycle stage metrics, contact growth, and email performance data for the reporting period.

DFC Load Sheet Generator.action: Accepts uploaded MHM vehicle part number data and the New Availability Guide directly in the browser, then matches part numbers across both files.

Super Session Saturday Automation.action: Monitors a designated Teams channel for a new Excel file uploaded by William Nguyen each week.

DFC SEO Blog System.action: Generates fully written B2B SEO blog posts targeting DFC's core audiences — shops, distributors, and fleets — based on keyword and topic inputs.

Friction Friday Automation.action: Scrapes YouTube for existing Friction Friday-style videos on the target vehicle, pulls reference images from Bing Images and Wikimedia Commons, and fetches vehicle background data from Wikipedia to ground the script in accurate specs and context.

Friction Friday Automation.action: Converts the Claude-generated script into a studio-quality voiceover audio file using the show's designated AI voice profile.

Friction Friday Automation.action: Receives the completed package — script text, voiceover file link, thumbnail asset, and assembled video reference — via an Apps Script webhook and logs the output with vehicle metadata for tracking and retrieval.

DFC HubSpot Data Setup.action: Extracts Dream CustomerIDs and associated customer data for all existing DFC accounts from the ERP system.

Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.action: Mark submits a vehicle specification — make, model, year, and any additional fitment details — through a HubSpot form, triggering the downstream workflow automatically.
Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.impact: The request is captured in a structured, machine-readable format from the start, eliminating back-and-forth emails and ensuring no spec detail is missing before processing begins.

Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.action: Queries the Dream ERP using the submitted vehicle specs to retrieve all parts DFC currently stocks that match that fitment, along with inventory levels and part numbers.
Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.impact: A complete picture of what DFC has for that vehicle is pulled instantly from the source of truth, replacing a slow manual lookup process that would otherwise require warehouse or system access.

Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.action: Compares the Dream inventory results against the full expected parts catalog for that vehicle to identify what DFC stocks and what is absent from inventory, then formats the findings into a structured Excel-ready output.
Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.impact: Gaps in DFC's catalog for a given vehicle are surfaced automatically and clearly, giving Mark actionable procurement or sales intelligence without manually cross-referencing two lists.

Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.action: Emails Mark the completed Excel file showing stocked and missing parts for the requested vehicle, ready for use in a special markets sales conversation.
Special Markets Catalog Maker (Mark Proj.impact: Mark receives a professional, ready-to-share catalog file within minutes of submitting his request, cutting a process that previously took hours of back-and-forth coordination down to near zero wait time.

Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Pulls live inventory status for every part on the weekly backorder report — including units on hand, on order, and in transit — and flags any parts that have no fulfillment source available.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: Every backorder line is enriched with real-time stock intelligence in one automated pass, replacing a process where a dozen people independently looked up the same information across disconnected systems.

Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Receives the enriched inventory data and merges it with the existing backorder report, producing an updated file where each line now shows current fulfillment status and dead-end flags inline.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: The team has a single enriched report to work from rather than scattered individual lookups, making triage faster and ensuring everyone is working from the same information.

Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).action: Generates and sends draft customer communications for flagged backorder lines — including estimated timelines where available and honest status updates for dead-end parts — ready for review or direct send.
Order Status Tracker (Mark Project 2).impact: Customer-facing updates are drafted automatically for every affected order, reducing the communication burden on the team and ensuring customers receive timely, accurate status information without delays.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Scrapes the online catalogs of Gyro Disc, Alcon, and Brembo to extract make, model, year, and part fitment data for all two-piece rotor offerings across each brand.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: A comprehensive multi-brand fitment reference is built automatically from live catalog data, replacing days of manual catalog review with a process that completes in minutes.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Organizes the scraped fitment data into a structured reference sheet and cross-references each entry against DFC's existing parts catalog to identify overlaps and gaps.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: Dino receives a clean, formatted catalog that immediately shows where DFC has coverage and where opportunity exists, without any manual data entry or formatting work.

Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.action: Sends automated outreach emails to relevant customers or prospects asking about their demand for specific two-piece rotor fitments identified in the catalog gap analysis.
Two Piece Rotor Catalog Builder.impact: Demand data is gathered directly from the market in parallel with catalog building, giving Dino real purchase intent signals to prioritize which gaps are worth filling first.

Meta Investigation.action: Pulls all active Meta and TikTok ad copy — including headlines, primary text, descriptions, and CTAs — across every live ad in the account for phrase scanning.
Meta Investigation.impact: Every active ad is reviewed simultaneously in one automated sweep, catching flagged language that a manual review would miss or take hours to find across large account structures.

Meta Investigation.action: Scans all pulled ad copy for the specified target phrases, identifies exact matches and close variants, and logs the ad ID, field name, and verbatim copy where each match was found.
Meta Investigation.impact: Flagged language is pinpointed with surgical precision — exact field and exact text — so the team can act immediately without re-reading every ad themselves.

Meta Investigation.action: Sends a formatted report email listing every flagged ad with the specific field, matched phrase, and full copy snippet for each instance found across Meta and TikTok.
Meta Investigation.impact: Compliance or copy issues are surfaced and delivered to the right person instantly upon request, enabling fast remediation and reducing the risk of policy violations or brand inconsistency going unnoticed.

summary: The AI automation pipeline is in strong shape this week, with 55 total agents across the board — 27 completed, 11 in progress, and 17 planned. No new agents were added this week, and no agents changed stages, so the pipeline remains steady from last week. The team continues to make solid progress on the 11 agents currently in development, and the growing library of 27 completed agents is actively delivering value across email marketing, SEO, paid media, sales, and creator outreach. Great work all around — we'll keep pushing forward on the in-progress builds and moving planned agents into development!
